name: Norwegian Flatbread
recipeIngredient: 2 small Potatoes, boiled or baked and pureed in the blender or food processor (about 1/2 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. It is impossible to find a more spartan cracker than Norwegian Flatbread. This flatbread can be made with only rye flour, salt, and water, but adding a pureed vegetable, such as potatoes, increases the nutritive value and adds subtle flavor. The flatbread is excellent with mild cheeses.
2. In a large bowl or in the food processor, blend the pureed potato, flour, and salt together.
3. Add the water and blend well until the dough will hold together in a cohesive ball.
4. Divide the dough into 10 equal portions.
5. Turn each portion out onto a floured surface one at a time and roll into circles. Roll as thin as possible, no more than 1/8 inch thick, so that each cracker is approximately 7 to 8 inches across.
6. Prick all over with the tines of a fork.
7. Lightly oil a griddle or large skillet and heat until very hot.
8. Place the rolled circles onto the griddle one at a time and cook until the bottoms begin to show light browning.
9. Turn and cook the other side, continuing to turn as needed until the whole cracker is dry and crisp. Do not allow the flatbread to get more than medium brown.
10. Cool on a rack. Yield: 10.
Variations:
- Substitute any pureed vegetable for potatoes.
- Use flours other than rye, such as wheat or barley.
- If the dough is too sticky to roll, add a little more flour. Letting the dough rest for an hour after mixing will also make it easier to roll.
- The flatbread can be cooked in the oven by baking on an ungreased baking sheet at 400°F for 8 to 12 minutes, or until crisp, turning 2 or 3 times.
